articles.title: Citgo sign goes dark — temporarily
articles.description:
The iconic landmark is being moved eastward and slightly higher.

The post Citgo sign goes dark — temporarily appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Citgo sign temporarily goes dark during relocation process.
Sign will be moved eastward and raised slightly for visibility.
Local businesses near Kenmore Square may experience reduced foot traffic.

articles.title: Canton police respond to 2 ‘significant incidents’ at controversial therapy center
articles.description:
A disturbance at the Judge Rotenberg Educational Center — infamous for its use of a divisive treatment — sent two residents and one police officer to the hospital Wednesday. 

The post Canton police respond to 2 ‘significant incidents’ at controversial therapy center appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Canton police responded to two significant incidents at the Judge Rotenberg Educational Center.
Two residents and one police officer were hospitalized following the disturbances on Wednesday.
The center's controversial treatment methods continue to raise safety concerns among local residents.

articles.title: Here’s what Boston meteorologists are saying about ‘unsettled weather’ into next week
articles.description:
"FRIDAY is decent… then it goes downhill."

The post Here’s what Boston meteorologists are saying about ‘unsettled weather’ into next week appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston will experience unsettled weather starting Saturday, May 30.
Meteorologists predict significant rain and wind through next week.
Residents should prepare for potential disruptions in outdoor activities.

articles.title: Former home-care aide charged in multimillion-dollar jewelry heist from JP home
articles.description:
The individual items stolen ranged from $1,000 to $850,000, police say.

The post Former home-care aide charged in multimillion-dollar jewelry heist from JP home appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Former home-care aide stole jewelry worth millions from a Jamaica Plain residence.
Stolen items valued between $1,000 and $850,000, highlighting significant local theft.
Residents urged to secure valuables following this high-profile crime in the community.

articles.title: NBA insider’s take on the Celtics-Giannis Antetokounmpo trade rumors
articles.description:
"That’s out there. I’m not saying that it hasn’t happened, but I haven’t heard it."

The post NBA insider’s take on the Celtics-Giannis Antetokounmpo trade rumors appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: NBA insider comments on Celtics' potential trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo.
No confirmation of trade rumors as of May 29, 2026.
Boston fans remain uncertain about the team's future roster changes.

articles.title: Human remains found in Worcester
articles.description:
The remains were found on Brandt Lane. An investigation is ongoing.

The post Human remains found in Worcester appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Human remains discovered on Brandt Lane in Worcester.
Investigation launched by local authorities following the discovery.
Residents urged to remain vigilant as investigation continues.

articles.title: Trooper in cruiser struck while working detail on I-93
articles.description:
The trooper suffered "minor injuries," police say.

The post Trooper in cruiser struck while working detail on I-93 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Trooper was struck while in cruiser on I-93 during a work detail.
The trooper sustained minor injuries but is expected to recover.
Incident highlights ongoing safety concerns for law enforcement on busy highways.

articles.title: A look at Kathie Lee Gifford’s $100 million Connecticut home
articles.description:
Kathie Lee Gifford and her late husband, football legend Frank Gifford, purchased the house in 1994.

The post A look at Kathie Lee Gifford’s $100 million Connecticut home appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kathie Lee Gifford's Connecticut home is valued at $100 million.
Gifford and her late husband purchased the property in 1994.
The high-value property reflects the luxury real estate market's growth affecting nearby Boston.

articles.title: Scooter rider and cyclist are killed in head-on crash on NYC bridge
articles.description:
The collision on the Queensboro Bridge involved an e-scooter that is not legally allowed to be used on the streets of New York.

The post Scooter rider and cyclist are killed in head-on crash on NYC bridge appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Two individuals were killed in a head-on crash on the Queensboro Bridge.
The e-scooter involved is illegal for street use in New York City.
This incident raises safety concerns for Boston's growing e-scooter usage.

articles.title: Boston still runs on Dunkin’. But a new coffee culture is taking hold.
articles.description:
The city’s newest cafe operators are trying to build a new coffee boom, even as the economics increasingly stack against them

The post Boston still runs on Dunkin’. But a new coffee culture is taking hold. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: New cafes in Boston aim to establish a vibrant coffee culture amid economic challenges.
Dunkin' remains dominant, but local coffee shops are emerging to attract diverse clientele.
Residents may experience a shift in coffee options and community engagement as new cafes open.
